verdict.evidence: The source says 'The Ministry of Defence is sending a proposal... for a consultation round' (present progressive, ongoing/imminent), while the output says 'The Ministry of Defence has sent a bill... to a public-consultation round' (present perfect, completed). This shifts the temporal framing from an action in progress to one already completed. All other temporal elements (deadline of 2 April 2026, future conditional tenses, present-tense descriptions of current law) are accurately preserved.
